immediate_actions: Open field drainage channels immediately
Remove standing water within 24 hours using pumps if needed
Avoid applying fertilizer on waterlogged soil
Monitor for fungal disease outbreak after water recedes
wheat.emergency_chemical: Spray Propiconazole 0.1% after water recedes to prevent rust
wheat.recovery_advice: Wheat can recover if waterlogged for under 48 hours at vegetative stage. Flag leaf and beyond is critical.
wheat.insurance_note: File crop damage report with local agriculture department within 72 hours

immediate_actions: Drain field within 12-24 hours — cotton roots die quickly in waterlogged soil
Cease all irrigation immediately
Apply potassium fertilizer after drainage to restore root function
Scout for boll rot and whitefly surge after flooding
cotton.emergency_chemical: Spray copper oxychloride 3g/L against boll rot after water drains
cotton.recovery_advice: Cotton at boll development stage cannot recover from >24 hours waterlogging. Significant yield loss expected.
cotton.insurance_note: Cotton is highest priority for flood damage claims. Document with photos.

immediate_actions: Rice tolerates flooding — no panic for first 5-7 days
Ensure water does not exceed plant height for more than 3 days
Drain if water is turbid/murky (reduces photosynthesis)
Watch for submergence beyond 7 days which can kill plants
rice.emergency_chemical: No emergency chemical needed unless disease appears
rice.recovery_advice: Rice is naturally tolerant. Varieties like Sub1 can survive 2 weeks submergence.
rice.insurance_note: Submit claims only if submergence exceeds 7+ days

immediate_actions: Drain field within 24-36 hours urgently
Create temporary drainage furrows between rows
Apply nitrogen fertilizer after drainage to compensate leaching
Check for stalk rot — major risk after flooding
maize.emergency_chemical: Carbendazim 50 WP 1g/L spray on base of stalks to prevent stalk rot
maize.recovery_advice: Maize is very sensitive at silking — even 36 hours can cause significant yield reduction.
maize.insurance_note: Photograph the flooding extent and submit claim within 72 hours

immediate_actions: Open drainage furrows along field edges
Avoid ratooning on waterlogged fields
Check for red rot infection after water drains
Do not apply urea until drainage is complete
sugarcane.emergency_chemical: Carbendazim 1g/L as soil drench after drainage to prevent red rot
sugarcane.recovery_advice: Sugarcane can tolerate moderate flooding. Main risk is red rot and root damage.
sugarcane.insurance_note: Document waterlogging duration and area affected

immediate_actions: Drain field immediately — sunflower has zero tolerance
Apply calcium nitrate 5kg/acre after drainage
Scout for Sclerotinia stem rot after flooding
Avoid mechanical operations on wet soil
sunflower.emergency_chemical: Carbendazim spray to prevent Sclerotinia after water recedes
sunflower.recovery_advice: Sunflower at flowering stage will likely not recover from >24 hours waterlogging.
sunflower.insurance_note: High value crop — prioritize insurance filing

immediate_actions: EMERGENCY: Drain within 12 hours — tubers rot rapidly
Pump water out if natural drainage is insufficient
Apply Metalaxyl-M fungicide after drainage to prevent late blight surge
Harvest early if tubers are mature — do not risk further losses
potato.emergency_chemical: Metalaxyl + Mancozeb spray (1g+2g per liter) as preventive measure
potato.recovery_advice: Potato cannot survive more than 12 hours waterlogging without significant losses.
potato.insurance_note: URGENT: File within 48 hours. High market value crop.

immediate_actions: Drain field immediately
Apply fungicide to prevent basal rot
Do not irrigate for 2 weeks after natural flooding
Harvest if 70% mature to salvage crop
onion.recovery_advice: Onion bulbs develop rot rapidly after flooding. Salvage harvest recommended.
onion.insurance_note: File claim with market price documentation

immediate_actions: Clear drainage around tree root zones
Apply fungicide drench to prevent Phytophthora root rot
Do not apply nitrogen fertilizer on wet soil
Monitor for fruit drop
mango.emergency_chemical: Metalaxyl soil drench to prevent Phytophthora root rot
mango.recovery_advice: Mature mango trees can tolerate short flooding. Young trees are more vulnerable.
mango.insurance_note: Document fruit drop count and tree damage for claims

immediate_actions: Open all field drainage channels immediately
Remove standing water as quickly as possible
Do not apply any fertilizer until soil drains
Contact local agriculture extension officer for crop-specific advice
default.emergency_chemical: Consult local agriculture department for crop-specific fungicide
default.recovery_advice: Monitor crop carefully for 7 days after flooding. Scout for disease.
default.insurance_note: File crop damage report with local agriculture department within 72 hours
